$comment: Mach-O EXECUTABLE (MH_EXECUTE) for x86_64 Darwin/macOS. Second Mach-O schema (plan 14 LK3 cycle 2, 2026-05-30). Consumed by `src/link/format/macho.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Differs from `macho64-x86_64-darwin.format.json` (the MH_OBJECT schema) in: `macho.filetype = 2` (MH_EXECUTE); `macho.flags` includes MH_PIE (0x200000) + MH_NOUNDEFS (0x1) + MH_DYLDLINK (0x4) + MH_TWOLEVEL (0x80) = 0x200085 (modern macOS exec convention); sections carry `virtualAddress` (loaded at ImageBase + offset); top-level `entryPoint` names the LC_MAIN entry-function symbol; new `image` block carries __PAGEZERO size + LC_LOAD_DYLINKER path + LC_LOAD_DYLIB list.
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit linux/darwin are LP64 (int 32, long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I64 here. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: macOS x86_64 generic LSB-first bit-field allocation == gnu_packed (Apple does not diverge from generic AAPCS64 on bit-field packing). Byte-ABI-exact to clang. See macho64-x86_64-darwin for the divergence-list rationale.
$longDoubleFormatComment: FC17.9(e) D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE: macOS x86_64 `long double` is the x87 80-bit extended format stored 16/16 (the SysV-x86_64 rule; Apple's x86_64 ABI follows SysV here). The axis resolves the c-subset row to F80 — declarations/layout/sizeof work; VALUE arithmetic walls loud until D-CSUBSET-LONG-DOUBLE-X87-ARITH lands.
$entryPointComment: Empty defaults to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention). LC_MAIN.entryoff is computed as (entry_function_offset_in_.text) — relative to the __TEXT segment start, NOT to file offset.
$externCallDispatchComment: DIRECT-PLT (corrected from the original mislabel). Mach-O resolves imports through a per-extern __stubs entry: the linker points each extern symbol's VA at its __stubs STUB (code) — see `symbolVa[extern] = stubVa` in macho.cpp — and the stub does the __got indirection internally (x86_64 `FF 25 disp32` jmp through the __got slot; ARM64 ADRP+LDR+BR x16). The call site is therefore a PLAIN DIRECT call (x86_64 `E8 disp32`, ARM64 `BL`) to the stub — byte-identical in shape to an intra-module call — NOT an `FF 15`-through-the-stub deref (that would dereference the stub's CODE bytes as a function pointer → SIGSEGV). This is the SAME direct-plt model ELF uses, and it matches what the trampoline + MIR→LIR `lowerCall` emit. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$processExitDeferredComment: D-LK10-ENTRY Slice B (plan 14 §2.13) intentionally OMITS `processExit` + `entryCallingConvention` here. macOS Mach-O exit mechanism is TBD per D-LK10-ENTRY-MACHO-EXIT: trade-off between (a) by-name-import via libSystem `_exit` (mirrors PE+ExitProcess but ties binary to dyld+libSystem loading) and (b) raw BSD syscall `0x2000001` (hermetic but Apple discourages — 'syscall numbers unstable across macOS versions'). Decision deferred to the first Mach-O runnable-binary trigger. Slice C's linker hook (`format.processExit().has_value()`) silently skips trampoline emission for this format until the choice is made + populated — intentional, not a defect.

macho.$comment: mach_header_64 identity. cputype = CPU_TYPE_X86_64 (0x01000007). cpusubtype = CPU_SUBTYPE_X86_64_ALL (3). filetype = 'execute' (MH_EXECUTE, closed enum). flags = MH_NOUNDEFS (0x1) | MH_DYLDLINK (0x4) | MH_TWOLEVEL (0x80) | MH_PIE (0x200000) = 0x200085 — modern macOS exec convention: no unresolved symbols at link time, uses dyld, two-level namespace lookup, position-independent.

image.$comment: Mach-O image fields for MH_EXECUTE. pageZeroSize = 0x100000000 (4 GiB) — the __PAGEZERO segment placed at vmaddr=0 catches null-pointer derefs at the kernel level. dylinkerPath = '/usr/lib/dyld' — the dynamic linker macOS always uses. loadDylibs = ['/usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib'] — required for any user-space process (provides start function + libc + Mach traps). Future cycles add Foundation / AppKit / etc. as needed.
image.$bindNowComment: Eager binding — the v1 stance per plan 14 §5 risk row, parallel to ELF cycle 2b.2's DF_1_NOW choice. Emits LC_DYLD_INFO_ONLY with the immediate bind_off opcode stream resolved at load via the linker; lazy_bind_off stays zero. Lazy upgrade (lazy_bind opcode stream resolved on first call via dyld_stub_binder) anchored at D-LK6-13; until then, the walker fails loud on bindNow=false.

sections.$comment: __text within __TEXT segment. virtualAddress = 0x100000000 + 0x1000 = 0x100001000 — __TEXT loads immediately after __PAGEZERO at the page boundary. flags (in substrate `type` field) = S_REGULAR (0) | S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS (0x80000000) | S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS (0x00000400) = 0x80000400. addrAlign = 16 (x86_64 instruction alignment).
sections.name: __text

relocations.$comment: Mach-O image-side relocs reuse the same kinds as the .o schema (BRANCH/UNSIGNED_8/UNSIGNED_4). cycle-2 walker applies intra-module relocs in-place via the LK6 cycle 1 structured-formula triple; the relocation_info records are NOT emitted for MH_EXECUTE (dyld doesn't read .o-style relocs — it reads LC_DYLD_INFO chained-fixups, which arrive at LK6 cycle 2 paired with FFI). nativeId packs (type<<28)|(length<<25)|(pcrel<<24) — same as the .o schema. r_extern (bit 27) + r_symbolnum (bits 0..23) are walker-filled.
relocations.name: X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH
